name: ShareClick: en düşük input-lag'li software KVM transport optimizasyonu

description: Baseline: UDP input transport (postcard-encoded, seq-numbered, dedicated blocking socket). Loopback RTT median 11.75µs, one-way ~5.88µs — transport overhead negligible, LAN RTT will dominate.

asi.hypothesis: Dedicated blocking UDP socket + compact postcard framing gives microsecond-scale transport overhead
asi.finding: 5.88us one-way on loopback; transport is not the bottleneck, real gains will come from OS capture->inject path and coalescing at high polling rates
asi.arch: hybrid transport: UDP input channel + reliable bulk channel; seq numbers drop stragglers to avoid head-of-line blocking
asi.next_action_hint: implement input capture (macOS CGEventTap/rdev) + emulation (enigo), then edge-switching and coalescing

description: Feature milestone: real input layer wired. rdev capture (server) -> UDP coalesced batches -> enigo injection (client). Portable Key enum + keymap bridges macOS/Windows keycodes. serve/connect run loops. Latency unchanged (6.52µs, within noise) — this is functionality, not a perf change.

asi.hypothesis: Add rdev+enigo native input layer behind a `native` feature; keep transport core benchmarkable without OS permissions
asi.finding: Full native stack (enigo+rdev+arboard) compiles on macOS arm64; portable Key enum avoids cross-platform keycode mismatch; per-tick event coalescing prevents high-polling-rate jumpiness
asi.note: metric within noise as expected — feature commit kept to preserve code, not a latency win
asi.next_action_hint: add edge-switching + local input suppression on server, then clipboard sync (arboard) over bulk TCP channel, then file transfer
asi.caveats: runtime needs macOS Accessibility permission; enigo Insert key missing on macOS -> mapped to fallback

description: Feature: reliable bulk TCP channel (length-prefixed frames, nodelay) + bidirectional clipboard sync (arboard) with echo suppression, auto-reconnect. Wired into serve/connect. New TCP roundtrip test passes. Latency within noise (7.06µs).

asi.hypothesis: Add TCP bulk channel for clipboard/files without touching the low-latency UDP input path
asi.finding: Clipboard sync works over length-prefixed postcard frames; echo suppression via shared LastSeen prevents ping-pong loops; TCP nodelay set. Bulk frame roundtrip test green.
asi.note: feature commit, metric within noise; kept to preserve code
asi.next_action_hint: implement file transfer (FileBegin/Chunk/End already in protocol) reusing bulk channel; then edge-switching + local input suppression; then encryption
asi.known_limitation: per-connection watch thread leaks on reconnect (MVP single-client acceptable); image clipboard not yet handled

description: Feature: file transfer over bulk channel (FileBegin/Chunk/End, 64KiB chunks, offset writes, path-traversal sanitization). New `send-file` CLI + receiver wired into serve/connect (writes to ./received). Byte-for-byte loopback test passes. Latency stable 6.58µs.

asi.hypothesis: Reuse the reliable bulk TCP channel for chunked file transfer without touching the UDP input latency path
asi.finding: End-to-end file transfer verified byte-for-byte over loopback TCP (2xchunk+remainder). Offset-based writes + filename sanitization guard against traversal. send-file CLI works without native feature.
asi.note: feature commit; latency unchanged as expected
asi.next_action_hint: remaining goal items: seamless edge-switching + local input suppression (rdev unstable_grab / CGEventTap), then encryption (X25519+ChaCha20). Core file+clipboard+input MVP now functional.
asi.verification: cargo test -p shareclick: bulk_frames_roundtrip_over_tcp ok, file_transfers_over_bulk_channel ok

description: Feature: real control handoff. Switched capture from passive listen to rdev grab so local input is SWALLOWED while remote is active. F12 hotkey toggles control between machines. This makes it a true KVM (not an input mirror). README updated. Tests pass, latency stable 6.67µs.

asi.hypothesis: Use rdev grab (unstable_grab feature) with an active flag + toggle hotkey to consume local input during handoff, turning the mirror into a real KVM
asi.finding: Compiles on macOS with grab; F12 toggles Arc<AtomicBool> active; when active, events forwarded+consumed (return None), when inactive passed through (Some). All tests pass, latency unchanged.
asi.goal_status: Core MVP complete: input sharing + control handoff + clipboard + file transfer all implemented; transport benchmarked ~6.7us; verifiable paths (transport, bulk frames, file transfer) tested green.
asi.runtime_note: grab needs macOS Accessibility permission; cannot exercise runtime handoff in sandbox but logic + compile verified
asi.next_action_hint: optional: automatic edge-switching without hotkey, encryption (X25519+ChaCha20), mDNS discovery, clipboard images
